diff --git a/CEO_NOTES.md b/CEO_NOTES.md
index 23bcfb0..b1bebb3 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -147,2 +147,20 @@
   - Ads scaled to S2 content $8,000/day.
 - Watch next week: A $79 conversion, tier 3 capacity headroom, compute cost under higher quotas, issue age, churn rate, and renewal cash.
+
+## Week 10 / Day 63
+
+- Cash: $538,852. Subscribers: 11,334. Week 9 added 4,898 subscribers from 8,436 leads; cash increased by ~$175k.
+- New S2 conversion at A $79 still worked: content S2 produced 7,756 leads from $56,000 and 4,483 paid S2 content subscribers. Subscription payments were $367,852.
+- Costs: ops -$96,600, ads -$56,000, compute -$17,577, capacity -$9,310, dev -$9,100. Growth and renewals now comfortably cover operating spend.
+- Active mix: S2 A 11,146, S2 B 122. A is still the volume product.
+- Issues: 869 open; 867 S2, mostly fresh, plus S1 has older issues up to 24 days. Cancellations rose to 65 but remain low relative to base and mostly old low-price cohorts.
+- Social still complains about quota limits despite very high quotas, suggesting heavy S2 professionals are using much more than expected. Higher quotas and enterprise-style pricing/limits may be needed later.
+- Changes:
+  - Started R&D tier 2: cost $333,333, completion day 93, expected boost +0.0619.
+  - Capacity raised to tier 4 (8M units/day) before another demand surge.
+  - New prices: A $89, B $229, C $399. S2 promo remains $10, so new S2 A cash is $79.
+  - Quotas raised to A 5000, B 20000, C 50000.
+  - Ops increased to $21,000/day total: global $6,000, S1 $2,500, S2 $12,000, S3 $500.
+  - Dev remains $1,300/day maintenance.
+  - Ads scaled to S2 content $12,000/day.
+- Watch next week: A $89 conversion, cash rebuild after R&D, tier 4 headroom, support backlog age, churn, and compute cost under larger quotas.
